sections.label: Lyrics
lines: [Verse 1]
They count your scars, they mark your fall,
But they don’t see you standing tall.
The fight is real, the nights are long,
But every bruise becomes a song.
You’re not the sum of all your slips,
There’s glory in your fingertips.
Each stumble just a stepping stone,
To take the crown and claim your own.
[Pre-Chorus]
Every fall rewrites my name
Grace and grit, not guilt or shame.
I don’t stay down, I don’t retreat,
The rhythm starts with a heartbeat.
[Chorus]
I fall, I rise — seven times and more
Knocked down, but I walk through every door
Grace lifts me every time I break
I bend, I burn, but never shake
‘Cause I was made to rise again
Even stronger than I was then
[Verse 2]
I've heard the lies that say I'm done,
But every ending fuels the run.
The dust don’t mean you’ve lost the war
It’s just the ground you’ll rise up from.
Faith ain’t just for mountaintops,
It’s forged in fire when all else stops.
So count me out, I’ll prove you wrong,
My weakness makes me twice as strong.
[Verse 3]
And when they see me rise once more,
They’ll find their strength inside the storm.
My scars will light a better way,
For those who fall but choose to stay.
I rise not just for me alone,
But for the ones still crawling home.
Each time I stand, I build a road
A legacy of hope I sowed.
[Bridge – Rhythmic and Reflective]
Seven steps to hit the ground,
Eight to rise and shake the sound.
Failure isn’t where I stay
It’s the forge that clears the way.
[Final Chorus – Full Power]
I fall, I rise — seven times and more
Knocked down, but I walk through every door
Grace lifts me every time I break
I bend, I burn, but never shake
‘Cause I was made to rise again
Even stronger than I was then
Even stronger than I was then…
